topic: Reaction to alternative No7 beauty and make-up campaign

stimulus_context.description: Respondents were shown an alternative No7 campaign intended to communicate the brand promise for its beauty and make-up range.
stimulus_context.shown_text: Now we want to show you a couple of alternative versions that No7 has put together to sum up their promise for their range of beauty and make up products. Please have a good read through their version.

question_context.main_question: What made you give it that score? Please be as specific as you can about which elements you find appealing or unappealing.
question_context.question_purpose: To understand the respondent’s reasoning, feelings, and interpretation of the manifesto in relation to the resonance score they gave.

objective.primary: Understand why the manifesto does or does not resonate with the respondent.
secondary: Identify which specific elements feel appealing
Identify which specific elements feel unappealing
Understand how the wording, tone, and presentation are being interpreted
Understand what is driving the score the respondent gave

routes.label: low_score
routes.focus: Explore what feels unappealing, unconvincing, unclear, or not resonating.

routes.label: mid_score
routes.focus: Explore both what works and what does not, and understand mixed or qualified reactions.

routes.label: high_score
routes.focus: Explore what feels appealing, convincing, and resonant.

seek: specific aspects of tone or language
specific elements of presentation or design where relevant
how the message is being interpreted
why it feels appealing or unappealing
what is driving the resonance score
avoid: generic follow-up prompts with no anchor
fact-checking or treating the response as right or wrong
trying to catch the respondent out
overly abstract probing that is not tied back to the manifesto or the question

language: Which words or phrases stood out to you?
Was there any wording that particularly worked or didn’t work for you?
Were there any parts of the wording that felt especially strong, weak, clear, or off?
visuals: Was there anything in the look, layout, or presentation that stood out to you?
Did any visual or design elements affect how you felt about it?
meaning: What did it suggest or imply to you?
What about it felt right or not quite right?
How did you interpret what No7 was trying to say?
personal_relevance: Why did that feel relevant or not relevant to you personally?
What made it connect or not connect with you?
Did it feel like it was speaking to someone like you? Why or why not?

completion.principle: Continue probing until the respondent’s thinking and feeling are clearly understood and sufficiently explored, not just until one specific point is identified.
minimum_requirements: The respondent has explained what they think
The respondent has explained why they think it
At least one concrete example, word, phrase, or identifiable element has been discussed
depth_expectations: More than one aspect should be explored where relevant
The reasoning should feel developed rather than partial
The answer should move beyond surface opinion where possible
stop_when: The respondent’s view is clear and well articulated
The main drivers of their score have been uncovered
Further probing is not adding meaningful new understanding
continue_when: The answer is still vague or generic
Only one narrow point has been explored and other relevant angles remain
The respondent’s reasoning is still unclear
There is clear room to deepen specificity or interpretation
